name: Home move
goal: Complete a residential move with housing secured, utilities and mail handled, belongings packed safely, and the first week in the new place survivable.
description: Four-week template from “we’re moving” to box drop-off. Skip tasks that do not apply (e.g. selling if you rent both ends). Adjust dates to your closing or lease start.

tasks.title: Lock dates, budget, and move type
tasks.content:
Decide the skeleton before you book trucks or cleaners.

Confirm:
- Move-out and move-in dates (include buffer for overlap or storage if needed)
- Total budget: movers, boxes, travel, deposits, overlap rent, cleaning, tips
- DIY vs hired movers vs hybrid; elevator or parking reservations if applicable

Admin:
- If selling/buying: key handoff dates with agent or landlord
- If renting: notice period, inspection expectations, deposit return process

Done when:
- Calendar holds exist for move day and any access windows
- A one-page move doc lists dates, addresses, and who is responsible for what

tasks.title: Housing transition: search, sale, or lease logistics
tasks.content:
Parallel path: secure the destination and unwind the origin.

Destination:
- Signed lease or closing checklist; utility account setup windows
- Measurements for large furniture; photos of rooms for placement planning

Origin:
- Listing or move-out checklist; repairs or paint if required
- Donation or disposal plan for what is not moving

Done when:
- You know where you sleep on night one after the move
- Keys, fobs, and access instructions are documented for both sides

tasks.title: Utilities, address change, and services
tasks.content:
Avoid living without hot water or missing bills.

Utilities:
- Power, gas, water, trash—schedule start at new address and stop at old (overlap if needed)
- Internet: install appointment often books weeks out

Address:
- USPS forward; banks, payroll, insurance, subscriptions, voter registration if relevant
- Vehicle registration / driver license updates if you changed state or country rules apply

Services:
- Cancel or transfer cleaning, lawn, security, HOA notices

Done when:
- A checklist with checkmarks lives in your move doc
- Critical services have confirmation numbers or appointment times

tasks.title: Packing plan, supplies, and valuables
tasks.content:
Pack by room and by “open first” priority, not by random box count.

Supplies:
- Boxes, tape, markers, bubble wrap, wardrobe boxes if worth it
- Color labels or numbers mapped to a room legend

Strategy:
- One or two “first night” boxes: bedding, toiletries, chargers, basic kitchen, tools
- Pack documents, jewelry, and backups separately; keep them with you
- Photos of cable runs and furniture assembly before disassembly

Done when:
- Non-essential rooms are boxed; essentials are staged for last-day pack
- Donation and trash runs are complete so you are not moving clutter

tasks.title: Move week: execution, cleanout, and settle-in
tasks.content:
The goal is a safe move and a returnable old place.

Day-of:
- Confirm mover arrival; protect floors and doorframes; final meter readings if required
- Inventory high-value items; tip and review if using pros

Old home:
- Final clean; return keys per checklist; photograph condition if disputes are possible

New home:
- Beds and essentials first; smoke detectors and main water shutoff located
- Change locks if policy allows; update delivery address on pending orders

Done when:
- Old keys returned; deposit or closing items submitted
- You can cook a simple meal and sleep in the new place without hunting for basics
